name: Careerspan Sales Prospecting Engine
description: System that surfaces recruiters discussing problems Careerspan solves, enabling V to engage constructively and warm them into sales conversations.
objective: 5 qualified NYC/SF recruiter prospects per day, surfaced with context and suggested engagement angles
key_decisions: Separate project from TLE, shares x_api.py via symlink
SQLite database for prospects and tweets
Topic-based discovery (pain points) over bio-based search
NYC/SF geo focus via bio keywords and location signals
Basic tier X API: 10K tweets/mo budget → ~300/day → be selective with queries
Qualification scoring: recruiter signals + pain alignment + engagement potential
Human-in-the-loop: V reviews and sends all engagements manually

icp_definition.who: Small to medium recruiters (agency or in-house)

careerspan_value_props.visibility: More visibility into candidate quality than job boards
careerspan_value_props.efficiency: Stop wasting time on unqualified applicants

workers.description: Create Projects/careerspan-prospector/ directory structure, symlink x_api.py, create config files
Create SQLite schema: prospects table, tweets table, engagements table, qualification_scores table
workers.schema_notes: prospects: id, username, bio, location, follower_count, recruiter_score, geo_match, discovered_at, status. tweets: id, prospect_id, text, pain_alignment_score, created_at. engagements: id, tweet_id, type, content, status, sent_at

workers.description: Create search_queries.yaml with pain-point searches and recruiter qualifiers, optimized for Basic tier budget
workers.query_strategy: Combine pain keywords with recruiter signals. Example: '(sourcing OR pipeline) (recruiter OR TA OR hiring) -filter:retweets'. Rotate queries to maximize coverage within 300/day budget.

workers.description: Create topic_monitor.py: loads queries, calls X API, dedupes, stores tweets and extracts prospect profiles
workers.implementation_notes: Use x_api.py search_recent_tweets(). Parse user objects from tweet responses. Check for existing prospects before inserting. Log API usage to stay within budget.

workers.description: Create icp_scorer.py: scores prospects on recruiter signals (bio keywords, job title patterns) and geo match (NYC/SF signals)
workers.scoring_logic: recruiter_score (0-100): keyword matches in bio, follower count normalization, account age. geo_score (0-100): location field + bio city mentions. Combined ICP score = weighted average.

workers.description: Create pain_classifier.py: classifies tweets by which Careerspan pain point they align with, scores alignment strength
workers.classification_notes: Map tweets to pain categories: quality, sourcing, visibility, efficiency. Use keyword matching + simple heuristics. Score 0-100 based on explicitness of pain expression.

workers.description: Create qualify_prospects.py: orchestrates ICP scoring + pain classification, ranks prospects, selects top 5 for daily review
workers.pipeline_logic: 1. Score all new prospects via ICP scorer. 2. Classify their tweets via pain classifier. 3. Compute composite score. 4. Filter to ICP threshold. 5. Rank and select top 5. 6. Update prospect status.

workers.description: Create engagement_generator.py: given a qualified prospect + tweet, generates constructive reply suggestions using Careerspan value props
workers.generation_notes: Use LLM (zo/ask API or local prompt) to generate 2-3 reply options. Tone: constructive, helpful, not salesy. Reference the specific pain point. Soft CTA if appropriate. V reviews and edits before sending.

workers.description: Create prospector_cli.py: CLI for V to run monitoring, view prospects, review engagements, mark sent/skipped
workers.cli_commands: scan (run topic monitor), qualify (run qualification), prospects (list qualified), review <id> (show prospect + suggestions), engage <id> (mark engagement sent), skip <id> (mark skipped), stats (usage/conversion)

workers.description: Create daily_digest.py: generates daily prospect digest (top 5) with context and suggested angles, can be scheduled as agent
workers.digest_format: Markdown report: 5 prospects, each with: profile summary, qualifying tweet, pain category, suggested engagement, link to tweet
